perturbation.original_text: 8. The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s undertakings herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s commitments herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The term "undertakings" is changed to "commitments". The agreement relies on the legal understanding of "undertakings," but introducing "commitments" without definition creates ambiguity. The contradiction arises because the remedies are tied to "undertakings," and it's unclear if they apply to these new, undefined "commitments." This would affect a court's interpretation of the agreement because "undertaking" has legal precedent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s commitments herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term "undertakings", which carries a legal understanding. The changed text replaces "undertakings" with "commitments". The contradicted text also uses the term "commitments". Since the remedies are tied to the legal understanding of "undertakings," but the altered text introduces the word "commitments" without clarifying if it is also subject to the same remedies, this creates a contradiction. A court might not interpret "commitments" as having the same legal weight as "undertakings", making enforcement of remedies ambiguous and contradictory, fulfilling the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
